questions.q: Maharaja Ranjit Singh is the founder of which empire?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh founded and ruled the Sikh Empire centred on the Punjab in the early 19th century.

questions.q: By what popular title is Ranjit Singh best remembered?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh is famously known as the Lion of Punjab, or Sher-e-Punjab.

questions.q: Which region did Ranjit Singh unify under his rule?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh unified the fragmented Sikh misls of the Punjab into a single empire.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh followed which religion?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh was a Sikh and built the Sikh Empire, though he ruled with notable religious tolerance.

questions.q: Which city served as the capital of Ranjit Singh's Sikh Empire?
questions.explanation: Lahore was the capital of Ranjit Singh's Sikh Empire.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh was famous for building what kind of military force?
options: An army of war elephants only
A force of foreign mercenaries alone
questions.explanation: He built a powerful, European-style modern army known as the Khalsa Army or Fauj-i-Khas.

questions.q: During Ranjit Singh's lifetime, the British were largely kept on which side of the Sutlej river?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh kept the British east of the Sutlej, his empire holding the lands to the west.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh ruled with a reputation for what attitude toward different faiths?
questions.explanation: He was known for religious tolerance, employing Hindus, Muslims and Europeans in his court and army.

questions.q: The famous Koh-i-Noor diamond came into Ranjit Singh's possession during his reign. Where did it pass after the empire fell?
questions.explanation: The Koh-i-Noor, held by Ranjit Singh, was surrendered to the British Crown after Punjab's annexation in 1849.

questions.q: What title did Ranjit Singh take that means 'great king'?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh assumed the title Maharaja when he established his sovereign rule over the Punjab.

questions.q: In which year did Ranjit Singh capture Lahore, his future capital, from its Bhangi misl rulers?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh took Lahore in 1799; he formally assumed the title of Maharaja two years later, in 1801.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh belonged to which Sikh misl before his rise to power?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh inherited leadership of the Sukerchakia Misl from his father Maha Singh.

questions.q: What was the name of Ranjit Singh's father, whom he succeeded as misl chief?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh's father was Maha Singh, leader of the Sukerchakia Misl, whom he succeeded as a boy.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh formally assumed the title of Maharaja in approximately which year?
questions.explanation: He was crowned Maharaja of the Punjab in 1801, consolidating his sovereign authority.

questions.q: Which renowned general served Ranjit Singh and led campaigns into the northwest frontier?
questions.explanation: Hari Singh Nalwa was Ranjit Singh's most celebrated commander, famed for campaigns in the northwest frontier.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's modernised infantry and artillery were trained partly by officers from which background?
questions.explanation: He hired European officers, including Frenchmen like Jean-Francois Allard and Jean-Baptiste Ventura, to modernise his army.

questions.q: Which important frontier region did Ranjit Singh's forces wrest from Afghan control?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh's empire annexed Peshawar, extending Sikh rule to the edge of Afghan territory.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh annexed which strategic valley region in the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh conquered Kashmir in 1819, adding the valley to the Sikh Empire.

questions.q: The 1809 Treaty of Amritsar fixed the boundary between Ranjit Singh's empire and which other power?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Amritsar (1809) set the Sutlej as the boundary between Ranjit Singh and the British East India Company.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh is associated with funding the marble-and-gold adornment of which major Sikh shrine?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh financed the gilding and marble of the Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple), giving it its famous golden appearance.

questions.q: After Ranjit Singh's death, his empire was annexed by the British following which conflicts?
questions.explanation: The Sikh Empire fell to the British after the Anglo-Sikh Wars, with final annexation in 1849.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's coins were struck not in his own name but in the name of what?
questions.explanation: His coinage, the Nanakshahi rupee, was struck in the name of the Sikh Gurus rather than his own.

questions.q: In which year did Maharaja Ranjit Singh die, after which the empire declined rapidly?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh died in 1839; the empire then weakened amid succession struggles and fell to the British by 1849.

questions.q: Who succeeded Ranjit Singh briefly as Maharaja immediately after his death in 1839?
questions.explanation: His eldest son Kharak Singh succeeded him in 1839, beginning a turbulent succession.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's elite household troops, trained on European lines, were known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Fauj-i-Khas was Ranjit Singh's elite, French-trained regular force at the core of his Khalsa Army.

questions.q: From which Afghan ruler did Ranjit Singh ultimately obtain the Koh-i-Noor diamond?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh acquired the Koh-i-Noor from the deposed Afghan ruler Shah Shuja Durrani around 1813.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh defeated Afghan forces at which 1813 battle, an early major victory near the frontier?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Attock in 1813 was a key Sikh victory over Afghan forces under Ranjit Singh's general Dewan Mokham Chand.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's last surviving son, who became the final Maharaja and was later exiled to Britain, was named what?
questions.explanation: Duleep Singh was the last Maharaja of the Sikh Empire and was exiled to Britain after annexation; the Koh-i-Noor was taken from him.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh captured the city of Multan in 1818 from which power?
questions.explanation: Multan was taken in 1818 from its Afghan-aligned Nawab, extending Sikh control over the lower Punjab.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's samadhi (memorial) stands in Lahore beside which major monument?
questions.explanation: The Samadhi of Ranjit Singh is located in Lahore adjacent to the Badshahi Mosque and Lahore Fort.
